intro: Mascagnia simsiana, also known as Sims' velvet tree, is a plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive, velvety leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Mascagnia simsiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever and respiratory issues, as well as being used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
